name: Banana Nut Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix 1 1/4 cups flour, 1/2 cup sugar, salt, and yeast.
2. Heat the evaporated milk, water, and 2 tablespoons margarine to 120°F-130°F. Do not melt the margarine.
3. Add the heated liquids to the dry ingredients; beat for 2 minutes at medium speed of the mixer.
4. Add eggs, mashed bananas, and 1 cup flour; beat until high speed for 2 minutes.
5. Stir in pecans and enough remaining flour to form a stiff dough. Knead for 8 to 10 minutes.
6. Grease the top of the dough and cover. Let rise until doubled, about 1 hour.
7. Punch down the dough. Divide into two portions; shape into loaves.
8. Place the shaped loaves in two greased loaf pans (8 1/2 x 4 1/2 x 2 1/2 inches).
9. Cover and let rise until doubled, about 1 hour.
10. Preheat oven to 375°F.
11. Mix remaining sugar with cinnamon. Brush the tops of the loaves with melted margarine and sprinkle evenly with the sugar mixture.
12. Bake for 25 to 35 minutes or until golden brown and firm when touched lightly. Cool.
